cover.title: THE GEM
cover.subtitle: The one who feels the truth early, then questions themselves in silence.
cover.tagline: You sense what others miss, but certainty comes only after you've doubted yourself first.

profile_scores.caption: High warmth and perceptiveness, low drive and expression. You sense things accurately, but in the end doubt yourself and stay silent.

short_summary: You notice the smallest shifts in tone, timing, and mood, but you often question yourself before trusting what you felt.
The pattern wasn't that you were too sensitive. It was that you kept believing someone else's explanation more than your own senses.
What changes next is not seeing people better. It's trusting yourself sooner.

full_report.headline: THE GEM
full_report.intro: You notice more than people think. The other person's expression, tone, reply speed, and small changes in mood rarely pass you by. But even when you feel something clearly, you often question yourself before you trust it. This report follows that pattern: why you sense things early, why you stay silent anyway, and what changes when you finally trust yourself.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You're someone who notices more than people think. The other person's expression. Their tone. How fast they reply. A mood that's different from usual.
Things other people pass by, you often notice quickly. But even while you feel something, you may not trust yourself right away.
"Am I being too sensitive?" "Am I reacting too strongly?" "Maybe it's nothing." You think it over one more time.
So people can find you unexpected. Your instincts arrive fast, while certainty takes longer.
Love can feel the same way. Interest shows up early, but you may need more time before you trust what it means.
chapters.profile_note: High warmth and perceptiveness, low drive and expression. You sense things accurately, but in the end doubt yourself and stay silent.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Repeat: THE SELF DOUBT CURVE
body: This pattern may feel familiar. Something felt off early. Something snagged. Something felt uncomfortable. Something did not quite add up.
But you let it go because there was no proof. Then time passed, and sometimes the concern you had at the start returned in a clearer form.
The thought afterward can sound like this: "I did feel something back then."
And when the next relationship begins, you may still give the other person's explanation more weight than your own discomfort.
chapters.highlight: The pain is not that you notice too much. It is that your own discomfort keeps getting pushed behind someone else's explanation.

chart.title: THE SELF DOUBT CURVE
chart.description: The pattern of sensing something, then doubting yourself, then eventually confirming it

chapters.title: What You Got Wrong About Yourself

items.truth: You may be more perceptive than you give yourself credit for. The question is not whether every feeling is correct. It is whether you keep dismissing feelings that deserve a closer look.
items.truth_short: You may be perceptive. What matters is whether you dismiss feelings that deserve attention.

items.belief: Wanting to be noticed is too much to ask.
items.truth: Wanting your effort to be noticed is not too much. Care feels safer when appreciation can be spoken instead of guessed.
items.truth_short: Wanting your effort to be noticed is a reasonable need.

items.belief: Let's just wait a little longer.
items.truth: Sometimes waiting gives you useful time. Sometimes it delays a conversation you already know you need to have.
items.truth_short: Waiting can help, but it can also delay a needed conversation.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: You can be drawn to people who express very little. People who do not say things easily, reveal much, or offer certainty quickly.
At first, they may seem deep. There can be something meaningful in slowly getting to know them.
But as time passes, you may find yourself guessing alone. The other person has not said much, and you keep trying to interpret the silence.
chapters.highlight: Someone who cares about you does not leave every feeling for you to figure out alone.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need isn't to see things even better. You already see plenty.
What you need is to trust your first feeling enough to pay attention to it.
If something feels uncomfortable or hurtful, you can pause there instead of explaining it away.
A feeling is not a verdict, but it is still information worth bringing into the conversation.
chapters.action_sentence: A feeling is not a verdict, but it is still information worth paying attention to.

chapters.title: Where to Go From Here
body: You know how to read people delicately. You know how to be considerate. You know how to think from the other person's perspective.
But what you'll learn next is how to think from your own perspective.
You already understand why they did what they did. Now it's time to look at how that action made you feel.
That is where the relationship has a chance to change.
chapters.transition_note: You've spent a long time understanding others first. The next step is letting your own experience have equal weight.

chapters.title: The Type You Keep Falling For

items.description: You ask how their day was, and they answer with one sentence and a smile. You may fill in the rest yourself. But mystery and emotional distance are not always the same thing.
They may notice your effort but rarely say it out loud. Every small word of recognition starts to feel precious, even though appreciation should not have to be earned.
They carry everything alone and rarely ask for help. It can look strong, but it may also keep closeness at a distance.

items.type_title: Someone Who Avoids Talking About Feelings
items.description: Serious conversations often end with, "I'm fine." Silence can hold depth, but it can also leave emotional responsibility on one side.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: You may have felt some of these signs early, then talked yourself out of them.

signal_strength.label: There's a moment that hurts, but you let it go right away
You interpret the other person's intent before you look at their actual behavior
You have less and less to say
You want to be noticed, but you still don't say anything

chapters.core_signal: You keep trying to understand, while the other person gets used to leaving more and more unsaid.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like to You

checklist.title: They don't hold back expression.
checklist.description: Their care does not depend on you guessing what they mean.

checklist.title: They say thank you.
checklist.description: Your effort is noticed and appreciated out loud.

checklist.title: They say it directly.
checklist.description: They can explain what happened and listen when something hurts.

checklist.title: There's no guessing game.
checklist.description: You feel steadier because the relationship does not require constant interpretation.

chapters.title: What Happens in Your Next Relationship (THE GEM)

sections.who_breaks_you: You're vulnerable to someone whose feelings stay hard to read. You may end up watching more, worrying more, and building meaning from very little. Over time, the relationship can leave you carrying both the feeling and the explanation.
sections.who_steadies_you: A steadier match is someone who says what they feel in simple, direct ways. They show appreciation, explain when something hurts, and do not make closeness depend on guessing.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You may still feel drawn to someone quiet or difficult to read. That does not mean the same pattern has to repeat. What matters is whether they meet your questions with openness, instead of leaving you alone with the silence.
sections.next_signs: This time, you may notice discomfort sooner. A small distance. A change in tone. A moment that leaves you unsure. The next step is not deciding what it means alone, but bringing the question into the relationship.
sections.try_this: When something feels unclear, ask directly and gently: "I might be reading this wrong, but is this what you meant?" Notice whether the conversation becomes clearer and whether both of you can stay present with the question.
sections.final_line: What matters is not one response. It is whether clarity and care keep growing between you.

action_plan.summary: You already know how to read people. What changes now is trusting what you felt before you talk yourself out of it.
action_items: When something feels off, write down the feeling before explaining it away.
Ask one direct question instead of interpreting the whole situation alone.
Treat hurt as information that deserves attention, not proof that you are wrong.

closing.message: You've spent a long time learning how to understand others. This report covered why you always end up understanding the other person first, and how you can learn to trust yourself more.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
